title: Night movement rule
summary: Practical guidance for “Night movement rule” within “Shelter · Power · Navigation”. It supports safe energy prioritisation, isolation of faults, ventilation, battery care, and communication continuity, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.
content:
Practical guidance for “Night movement rule” within “Shelter · Power · Navigation”. It supports safe energy prioritisation, isolation of faults, ventilation, battery care, and communication continuity, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.

Start with: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Night movement rule” in “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.

Stop condition: Never enter moving floodwater, wildfire closure, smoke-obscured road, unstable slope, damaged structure, or officially restricted route. Apply this boundary to “Night movement rule” in “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.

Reduced fallback: If “Night movement rule” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use a switched-off reserve device, non-electric method, or professionally checked power source.

steps: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Night movement rule” in “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.

Confirm the reason for movement, official instruction, destination, primary route, fallback route, weather, daylight, and transport limits. Record the result under “Shelter · Power · Navigation” before continuing “Night movement rule”.

Mark hazards such as fire, smoke, floodwater, damaged bridges, unstable slopes, crowds, poor visibility, and restricted areas. Recheck this point before closing “Night movement rule” for “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.

Match pace, load, rest, assistance, and transport to children, older adults, disability, illness, animals, and the slowest safe member. Treat any uncertainty here as an unresolved item in “Night movement rule” for “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.
Leave a departure time, route, destination, group list, check-in schedule, and missed-contact rule with a trusted person. A second person should be able to verify this part of “Night movement rule”.

Stop at planned checkpoints, compare real conditions with the map, and turn back or divert before the route becomes trapping or irreversible. Include the outcome of “Night movement rule” in the handover for “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.
warnings: Never enter moving floodwater, wildfire closure, smoke-obscured road, unstable slope, damaged structure, or officially restricted route. Apply this boundary to “Night movement rule” in “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.
Do not split the group without a written destination, communication rule, responsible adults, and reunification plan. Apply this boundary to “Night movement rule” in “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.

Treating the result as safe while ignoring this limit is a serious error: Never enter moving floodwater, wildfire closure, smoke-obscured road, unstable slope, damaged structure, or officially restricted route. Apply this boundary to “Night movement rule” in “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.

Apply this boundary to “Night movement rule” in “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.
alternatives: If “Night movement rule” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use a switched-off reserve device, non-electric method, or professionally checked power source.
Use a dated paper note and spoken read-back for “Night movement rule” in “Shelter · Power · Navigation” when the normal form, device, or communication channel is unavailable.
failure_signs: Stop and reassess “Night movement rule” when the route loses a safe exit, visibility, communications, daylight margin, water margin, or physical capacity. Apply this boundary to “Night movement rule” in “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.

Stop and reassess “Night movement rule” when a member is missing, distressed, injured, exhausted, confused, or unable to continue safely. Apply this boundary to “Night movement rule” in “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue “Night movement rule” while there is live electricity, damaged wiring, water near circuits, battery swelling or heat, fuel vapour, poor ventilation, or an unknown connection. Apply this boundary to “Night movement rule” in “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.

This card is not authorization to back-feed a building, bypass protection, open an energized device, charge a damaged battery, or run fuel-burning equipment indoors. Apply this boundary to “Night movement rule” in “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.

long_term: Review “Night movement rule” after real use in “Shelter · Power · Navigation”; replace failed materials, practise the handover, and update thresholds when official guidance or local conditions change.
if_method_fails: If the method described in “Night movement rule” gives no verifiable result, stop relying on it, isolate the uncertain output, record where it failed, and switch to a switched-off reserve device, non-electric method, or professionally checked power source.
environment_notes: Adapt the guidance in “Night movement rule” for “Shelter · Power · Navigation” to weather, lighting, crowding, accessibility, power, water, transport, and available skill without removing the stop condition or independent check.
